question:
Eight junior high school students were taken to a shopping mall. The number of socially inappropriate behaviors (faux pas) by each student was counted. The students were then enrolled in a program designed to promote social skills. After completing the programs, the subjects were again taken to the shopping mall and the number of social faux pas was again counted. Table $\mathbf{1 1 . 3}$ lists data from this experiment.
(a) Calculate the change in the number of faux pas within individuals (i.e., calculate DELTA for each observation).
(b) Plot the differences as a stemplot. Comment on the results.
(c) Would you use $t$ procedures on these data?
(d) Test the mean decline for statistical significance. Use a two-sided test. Show all hypothesis-testing steps.
answer:
(a) Data

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|}
\hline VISIT1 & VISIT2 & DELTA \\
\hline 5 & 4 & -1 \\
\hline 13 & 11 & -2 \\
\hline 17 & 12 & -5 \\
\hline 3 & 3 & 0 \\
\hline 20 & 14 & -6 \\
\hline 18 & 14 & -4 \\
\hline 8 & 10 & 2 \\
\hline 15 & 9 & -6 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(b) The stemplot is:

$$
\begin{gathered}
-0 \mid 566 \\
-0 \mid 124 \\
0 \mid 02 \\
\times 10
\end{gathered}
$$

- Six of the eight students $(75 \%)$ showed a decline in the number of faux pas.
- Data range from -6 to 2 (spread).
- The median is -3 (location).
- The distribution is mound shaped with no apparent outliers, and shows no dramatic departures from Normality.
(c) Yes, a $t$ procedure can be used (no major departures from Normality).
(d) Hypothesis test.

The four step procedure is applied:
A. Hypotheses. $H_0: \mu_d=0$ versus $H_a: \mu_d \neq 0$
B. Test statistic. $n=8, \bar{x}_d=-2.75, s_d=2.964, S E=\frac{2.4(x-4)}{\sqrt{8}}=1.048 t_{\text {stat }}=\frac{2.70-0}{1.048}=2.62$ with $d f=8-1=7$.
C. $P$-value. $P=0.034$ (via www.cytel.com/Products/StaTable/). Using Table C, $0.025<P<0.05$. These $P$-value provide good evidence against $H_0$.
D. Significance level (optiona). The difference is significant at $\alpha=0.05$ but not at $\alpha=0.01$.
